project.name: ruvector slice 4 — Attention & Min-Cut Stack
project.description: RuVector slice 4 — attention and min-cut algorithm crates (geometric/graph/sparse attention, subpolynomial dynamic min-cut, mincut-gated transformer inference, and HNSW-topology GNNs) built on slice-3 math/graph/DAG/CNN primitives. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for ruvector-attention, declaring dependencies and feature flags for the geometric/graph/sparse attention crate that hosts sheaf, sliced-Wasserstein, RoPE, and topology-gated variants.

nodes.name: scaled_dot_product.rs
nodes.summary: Reference scaled dot-product attention implementing the AttentionMechanism trait, serving as the baseline variant against which slice-4's geometric attention variants (sheaf, sliced-Wasserstein, topology-gated) are compared.

nodes.name: rope.rs
nodes.summary: Graph-aware Rotary Positional Embeddings (RoPE) that encode graph distances as rotation angles, providing positional bias for the attention stack when sequences carry an underlying graph topology.
nodes.languageNotes: Builder pattern (RoPEConfigBuilder) plus rotation-matrix application kept SIMD-friendly via flat f32 pair iteration.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for ruvector-attention that re-exports the full attention surface (sheaf, hyperbolic, info-geometry/bottleneck, MoE, PDE, sparse, topology, transport, training, curvature, graph) wired together as the slice-4 attention zoo.

nodes.name: attention.rs
nodes.summary: Sheaf attention variant computing Dirichlet energy via per-edge restriction maps and converting energies to attention weights, expressing the graph-attention framing as cohomology over a cellular sheaf.
nodes.languageNotes: Heads-by-restriction-map design lets queries/keys/values flow through independent linear maps before energy is computed.

nodes.name: gated_attention.rs
nodes.summary: Topology-gated attention that switches between full, sparse, and retrieval-only modes based on a coherence signal, providing the runtime knob that lets slice-4 trade attention width for compute under a topology budget.

nodes.name: sliced_wasserstein.rs
nodes.summary: Sliced-Wasserstein attention scoring keys against queries using 1D optimal-transport distances on random projections, giving slice-4 a distributional/geometric attention variant grounded in optimal transport.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-stage compute and window cache amortize OT projections; manual SIMD-friendly dot products avoid heap allocations on the hot path.

nodes.name: mincut.rs
nodes.summary: Dinic max-flow/min-cut solver and dynamic_min_cut wrapper that drive attention-side gating decisions for the attn-mincut crate, producing CutResult/GatingResult pairs consumed by slice-4's mincut-attention bridge.

nodes.name: mmap.rs
nodes.summary: Memory-mapped embedding and gradient storage backing the HNSW-topology GNN, providing AtomicBitmap-tracked dirty pages, prefetch hints, and sharded gradient accumulation for billion-scale message-passing.
nodes.languageNotes: AtomicBitmap drives both dirty-page flushing and prefetching, keeping mmap writeback bounded during HNSW neighborhood updates.

nodes.name: bounded_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benches measuring bounded-time insert/delete/query and mixed workloads for the subpolynomial dynamic min-cut, validating that update operations stay within the algorithm's amortised complexity envelope.

nodes.name: PAPER_IMPLEMENTATION.md
nodes.summary: Mapping note that links the ruvector-mincut implementation back to the original subpolynomial dynamic min-cut paper, listing which structures (J-tree, fragmenting, certificates) realise which paper sections.

nodes.name: 04-integration-guide.md
nodes.summary: Long-form integration guide describing how to embed ruvector-mincut into downstream crates (attention gating, gated transformer, GNN) with self-healing and certificate-audit workflows.

nodes.name: subpoly_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example that times the dynamic min-cut across growing graph sizes and verifies the empirically subpolynomial scaling claim against power-law fits.

nodes.name: audit.rs
nodes.summary: Append-only AuditLogger and AuditEntry types that record witness lifecycle, queries, and mincut changes, providing the verifiable certificate trail that powers self-healing recovery in the subpolynomial mincut.

nodes.name: fragment/mod.rs
nodes.summary: FragmentingAlgorithm that partitions the graph into connected components and computes per-fragment min-cuts (brute-force or heuristic), the geometric decomposition that keeps the subpolynomial algorithm's update cost local.

nodes.name: jtree/mod.rs
nodes.summary: J-tree module facade exposing coordinator/hierarchy/level/sparsifier submodules plus alpha and level-count helpers that parameterise the hierarchical sparsifier driving the subpolynomial mincut.

nodes.name: parallel.rs
nodes.summary: Work-stealing scheduler and parallel min-update primitives (ParallelLevelUpdater, ParallelCutOps) that fan the J-tree level updates and boundary/min-degree computations across rayon threads for low-latency mincut.

nodes.name: neuron.rs
nodes.summary: LIF spiking neuron, SpikeTrain, and NeuronPopulation primitives that translate min-cut activity into a biologically-flavoured spike code, feeding skip-ratio and synchrony signals into the gated transformer's coherence controller.

nodes.name: simd.rs
nodes.summary: WASM/SIMD intrinsics for popcount, XOR, batched membership, and boundary-size scans over compact bitsets, accelerating the mincut's hot inner loops on browser/edge deployments.

nodes.name: wrapper_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test suite cross-validating the dynamic min-cut wrapper against Stoer-Wagner reference cuts, including property tests for symmetry, monotonicity, geometric-range coverage, and fuzz/sequence determinism.

nodes.name: scorer.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end example that runs the gated transformer's sparse-attention scorer and reports per-tier latency, demonstrating ultra-low-latency inference with mincut gating active.

nodes.name: gate.rs
nodes.summary: GateController that fuses energy-gate and config signals into a TierDecision (full / reduced / safe / intervention), the coherence-control core that throttles KV writes and external effects in the gated transformer.

nodes.name: kv_cache/mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel that wires the gated-transformer KV cache submodules (hot buffer, tiered store, kivi/kvquant/squat quantizers, policy/metrics/manager) into one façade for the low-latency inference path.

nodes.name: sparse_attention.rs
nodes.summary: MincutSparseAttention that builds a sparse mask from mincut-derived partition boundaries with a lambda density schedule, delivering boundary-aware sparse attention for the gated transformer.

nodes.name: mod_routing.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering lambda-driven routing, tier-degraded capacity, boundary-edge concentration, and skip-ratio behaviour, exercising the gated transformer's coherence-control routing end to end.

nodes.summary: Speculative decoding implementation (Leviathan et al., 2023) using draft-verify rejection sampling and Medusa multi-head prediction for 2-3x inference speedup with zero quality loss in the attention stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements trait-based DraftModel/TargetModel abstractions plus Medusa heads; rejection sampling preserves exact target distribution.

nodes.name: approximate.rs

nodes.name: ApproxMinCutConfig

nodes.name: ApproxMinCut

nodes.name: ApproxMinCutResult

nodes.name: insert_edge

nodes.name: delete_edge

nodes.name: min_cut

nodes.name: is_connected

nodes.name: compute_effective_resistances

nodes.summary: Builds a spectral sparsifier with O(n log n / ε²) edges by sampling proportional to resistance × weight.

nodes.name: ensure_sparsifier_connectivity

nodes.name: stoer_wagner

nodes.name: compute_partition

nodes.name: certificate/mod.rs

nodes.name: WitnessSummary

nodes.name: LocalKCutResultSummary

nodes.name: UpdateType

nodes.name: CertificateError

nodes.name: verify
nodes.summary: Verifies a CutCertificate end-to-end: witnesses are valid, LocalKCut responses refute smaller cuts, hashes match.

nodes.name: with_witnesses

nodes.name: to_json

nodes.name: from_json
nodes.summary: Deserializes a CutCertificate from JSON, returning a CertificateError on invalid input.

nodes.name: fmt
nodes.summary: Display impl for CertificateError producing human-readable error messages for logs and audit trails.

nodes.name: fragmentation/mod.rs

nodes.name: FragmentationConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for fragmentation: expansion parameter φ, max fragment size, recursion depth limits.

nodes.name: Fragment
nodes.summary: A single fragment in the decomposition tree: its vertex set, boundary edges, and sparsity score.

nodes.name: TrimResult
nodes.summary: Result of the Trim subroutine: the trimmed vertex set, sparsity witness, and remaining boundary stats.

nodes.name: Fragmentation
nodes.summary: Recursive graph-fragmentation engine maintaining a hierarchy of expander-like fragments under dynamic insertions/deletions.

nodes.name: boundary_sparsity
nodes.summary: Computes the boundary-sparsity score of a fragment (boundary / volume) used as an expansion criterion.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Inserts a weighted edge and updates affected fragments in the hierarchy.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Deletes an edge and triggers refragmentation if it crosses or breaks an expander fragment.

nodes.name: fragment
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs the recursive fragmentation procedure on the current graph state.

nodes.name: try_fragment_recursive
nodes.summary: Recursive helper that attempts to fragment a region into smaller expander pieces, respecting depth limits.

nodes.name: create_fragment
nodes.summary: Constructs a Fragment from a vertex set, computes boundaries, and registers it in the hierarchy.

nodes.name: trim
nodes.summary: Trim subroutine that finds boundary-sparse cuts inside a putative expander, returning a refined vertex set.

nodes.name: is_expander
nodes.summary: Checks whether a fragment is an expander against the configured φ via the trim subroutine.

nodes.name: max_depth
nodes.summary: Returns the maximum depth of the fragmentation hierarchy for diagnostic/telemetry purposes.

nodes.name: sparsifier.rs
nodes.summary: Vertex-split-tolerant dynamic cut sparsifier (j-tree, arXiv:2601.09139): forest packing with lazy repair achieves O(log² n / ε²) recourse per vertex split while maintaining a (1±ε)-cut approximation.

nodes.name: SparsifierConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the j-tree dynamic sparsifier: ε approximation, number of forests, repair thresholds.

nodes.name: SparsifierStatistics
nodes.summary: Telemetry counters: edges sampled, vertex splits handled, repair operations, recourse totals.

nodes.name: VertexSplitResult
nodes.summary: Result of a vertex split: new vertex ids, recourse cost, and forests that needed repair.

nodes.name: RecourseTracker
nodes.summary: Tracks recourse per update against the theoretical O(log² n / ε²) bound for the j-tree paper.

nodes.name: Forest
nodes.summary: Single forest inside the forest packing: stores its edge set plus repair state.

nodes.name: ForestPacking
nodes.summary: Pack of O(log n / ε²) spanning-tree forests sampled to approximate every cut within (1±ε).

nodes.name: DynamicCutSparsifier
nodes.summary: Top-level j-tree dynamic cut sparsifier: insert/delete edge, split vertex, perform lazy repairs, query statistics.

nodes.name: is_within_bound
nodes.summary: Checks if cumulative recourse remains within the theoretical j-tree bound for the current graph size.

nodes.name: theoretical_bound
nodes.summary: Computes the O(log² n / ε²) theoretical recourse bound used as the regression guard.

nodes.name: sample_edge
nodes.summary: Samples a weighted edge across the forest packing, deciding which forest(s) should contain it.

nodes.name: repair_forest
nodes.summary: Repairs a single forest after splits/deletions break tree invariants.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Builds the j-tree sparsifier from an initial graph snapshot with the supplied configuration.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Inserts a weighted edge and updates the forest packing with bounded recourse.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Deletes an edge and schedules lazy repair on affected forests.

nodes.name: split_vertex
nodes.summary: Top-level vertex-split entry point on DynamicCutSparsifier: applies the partition, delegates to ForestPacking, and records recourse against the theoretical bound.

nodes.name: perform_lazy_repairs
nodes.summary: Runs all pending forest repairs in one pass to amortize cost across updates.

nodes.name: compression_ratio
nodes.summary: Reports the sparse-to-dense edge ratio for benchmark/telemetry output.

nodes.name: pool.rs
nodes.summary: Pool allocators and lazy level-deallocation strategies for the j-tree mincut stack: compact adjacency representation, demand-paged level materialization, vertex remapping, targeting 50-75% memory reduction.

nodes.name: PoolConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the level pool: max materialized levels, eviction threshold, prealloc size, lazy mode.

nodes.name: PoolStats
nodes.summary: Telemetry counters: cache hits/misses, evictions, materializations, current memory footprint.

nodes.name: LazyLevel
nodes.summary: Single lazily-allocated j-tree level: materialized flag, dirty flag, and slot for the inner LevelData.

nodes.name: LevelData
nodes.summary: Concrete data block backing a materialized level (graph snapshot, sparsifier state, last-touch counter).

nodes.name: CompactAdjacency
nodes.summary: Compact CSR-style adjacency list for small graphs (u32 offsets, u32 neighbors) used to shrink per-level memory.

nodes.name: LevelPool
nodes.summary: Central pool managing many LazyLevels with LRU-like eviction and on-demand materialization under a configurable cap.

nodes.name: CompactVertexMapper
nodes.summary: Maps original vertex ids to compact indices and back, enabling u16/u32 storage in small subgraphs.

nodes.name: from_edges
nodes.summary: Constructs a CompactAdjacency from an edge list and vertex count in one pass.

nodes.name: neighbors
nodes.summary: Returns the neighbor slice of vertex v in the compact adjacency representation.

nodes.name: with_config
nodes.summary: Constructs a LevelPool with the supplied PoolConfig and preallocates internal buffers.

nodes.name: materialize
nodes.summary: Materializes a previously-evicted level by loading LevelData and updating cache state.

nodes.name: evict
nodes.summary: Evicts a level to free memory, honoring dirty flags and the eviction threshold.

nodes.name: ensure_capacity
nodes.summary: Evicts old levels until the pool fits within the configured maximum materialized count.

nodes.name: touch
nodes.summary: Marks a level as recently used to keep it from being chosen for eviction.

nodes.name: allocate_level
nodes.summary: Reserves a new level slot and (optionally) eagerly materializes a buffer of the given capacity.

nodes.name: stats
nodes.summary: Builds a PoolStats snapshot for telemetry consumers.

nodes.name: get_or_insert
nodes.summary: Maps an original vertex id to its compact index, inserting a new mapping on first sight.

nodes.name: snn/optimizer.rs

nodes.name: OptimizerConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the neural graph optimizer: learning rate, replay capacity, exploration ε, STDP windows.

nodes.name: GraphAction

nodes.name: OptimizationResult
nodes.summary: Per-step optimization result: chosen action, TD error, mincut delta, value estimate.

nodes.name: Experience
nodes.summary: Single replay-buffer tuple: state, action, reward, next-state, priority.

nodes.name: PrioritizedReplayBuffer
nodes.summary: Prioritized experience replay buffer with priority-proportional sampling for stable learning.

nodes.name: ValueNetwork
nodes.summary: MLP value-function approximator estimating expected mincut improvement; TD-learned via spike timing.

nodes.name: PolicySNN

nodes.name: NeuralGraphOptimizer
nodes.summary: Top-level RL agent that owns the policy SNN, value network, replay buffer, and a DynamicGraph it learns to optimize.

nodes.name: estimate
nodes.summary: ValueNetwork forward pass mapping a graph feature vector to an expected mincut-improvement value.

nodes.name: new

nodes.name: run_until_decision

nodes.name: apply_reward_modulated_stdp

nodes.name: low_activity_regions

nodes.name: new_optimizer
nodes.summary: Constructs a NeuralGraphOptimizer over a given graph with policy/value networks and replay buffer wired up.

nodes.name: optimize_step

nodes.name: decode_action

nodes.name: apply_action
nodes.summary: Applies a GraphAction (add/remove/split/no-op) to the underlying DynamicGraph and returns the realized reward.

nodes.name: measure_search_efficiency
nodes.summary: Measures how many mincut-improving actions the agent finds per unit search step.

nodes.name: search
nodes.summary: Performs k-nearest skip-region search guided by the policy SNN's low-activity hints.

nodes.name: extract_features

nodes.name: estimate_mincut
nodes.summary: Cheap mincut estimate used as part of the feature vector and reward signal.

nodes.name: witness/mod.rs

nodes.name: EdgeWitness
nodes.summary: Per-tree-edge witness record: tree edge identifier, cut value at the time of witnessing, and cut side membership.

nodes.name: WitnessTree

nodes.name: LazyWitnessTree
nodes.summary: Lazy variant that batches updates until a threshold or query forces a flush, trading freshness for amortized cost.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Builds an initial WitnessTree from a graph snapshot by computing the spanning forest and witnessing each tree edge.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Inserts an edge into the eager witness tree, repairing affected witnesses where necessary.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Deletes an edge and, if it was a tree edge, finds a replacement and updates the witness for the affected cut.

nodes.name: is_tree_edge
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given (u, v) edge is currently part of the spanning forest.

nodes.name: find_witness
nodes.summary: Looks up the witness data associated with a tree edge.

nodes.name: recompute_min_cut
nodes.summary: Recomputes the current minimum cut from scratch when witnesses can no longer be repaired incrementally.

nodes.name: find_replacement
nodes.summary: Searches for a replacement tree edge after a tree edge is deleted, restoring spanning-tree invariants.

nodes.name: build_spanning_tree
nodes.summary: Builds the initial spanning tree underlying the witness tree using a BFS/Kruskal-style traversal.

nodes.name: flush_pending
nodes.summary: Flushes pending insert/delete operations in the LazyWitnessTree, materializing the current min cut.

nodes.name: LayerWeights

nodes.name: alloc_i32

nodes.name: alloc_bytes
nodes.summary: Reserves an arbitrary byte slice with caller-specified alignment, used as the building block for typed allocators.

nodes.name: calculate_arena_size
nodes.summary: Computes the arena capacity needed for a given (layers, hidden, ffn_mult, heads) configuration.

nodes.name: empty

nodes.name: bench_utils.rs

nodes.name: Timer
nodes.summary: Lightweight wall-clock/RDTSC timer with start/stop/reset, elapsed cycles/ns/μs accessors.

nodes.name: BenchStats
nodes.summary: Running benchmark statistics: min/max/mean/median/stddev nanoseconds plus peak and mean GFLOPS.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Canonical source-anchored min-cut: deterministic global cut value via repeated s-t cuts (Dinic max-flow), tamper-evident SHA-256 receipts, and a SourceAnchoredMinCut handle for incremental edge updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Pairs a low-level adjacency snapshot with a high-level handle; sha256 is inlined to avoid an external dependency on the receipt path.

nodes.name: SourceAnchoredCut
nodes.summary: Output struct describing a deterministic source-anchored cut: partition, cut value, and identifier of the source vertex.

nodes.name: SourceAnchoredConfig
nodes.summary: Knobs for the source-anchored solver: tie-breaking strategy, max iterations, and receipt-emission flag.

nodes.name: SourceAnchoredReceipt
nodes.summary: Tamper-evident receipt binding a cut to a graph epoch via SHA-256 of the canonical partition + edge set.

nodes.name: SourceAnchoredMinCut
nodes.summary: Stateful handle that owns the working graph, supports incremental insert/delete edge updates, and serves deterministic canonical cuts with optional receipts.

nodes.name: CanonicalMinCutResult
nodes.summary: Public result wrapping the canonical cut, its receipt, and stats consumed by the rest of the slice-4 pipeline.

nodes.name: global_mincut_value
nodes.summary: Computes the global min-cut value via repeated s-t cuts from a fixed anchor vertex, the source-anchored canonical algorithm.

nodes.name: min_st_cut
nodes.summary: Computes a single deterministic s-t min cut on the adjacency snapshot via Dinic max-flow.

nodes.name: dinic_maxflow
nodes.summary: Iterative Dinic blocking-flow loop with BFS level graphs and DFS augmenting paths.

nodes.name: sha256
nodes.summary: Inlined SHA-256 implementation used to keep receipt hashing dependency-free.

nodes.name: canonical_mincut
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point that runs the source-anchored algorithm and returns the cut plus a tamper-evident receipt.

nodes.name: polylog.rs
nodes.summary: Holm-Lichtenberg-Thorup style polylogarithmic dynamic-connectivity structure backing the mincut crate, with leveled spanning forests, replacement-edge search, and amortised rebuild.

nodes.name: PolylogConnectivity
nodes.summary: Main API for incremental edge insert/delete with polylogarithmic connectivity queries.

nodes.name: PolylogStats
nodes.summary: Stats snapshot (operations, rebuilds, level counts) for the polylog connectivity structure.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Adds an edge to the leveled forest, growing the spanning tree if needed and bumping the rebuild counter.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Deletes an edge and triggers replacement-edge search to restore the spanning forest if a tree edge was removed.

nodes.name: find_replacement
nodes.summary: Level-by-level non-tree-edge scan that finds the replacement edge after a tree-edge deletion.

nodes.name: rebuild_level
nodes.summary: Rebuilds a specific level of the leveled forest to amortise the cost of repeated replacement searches.

nodes.name: witness.rs
nodes.summary: Witness primitives proving membership in a cut: implicit witnesses (boundary + seed only), eagerly materialised Witness, and LazyWitness/LazyWitnessBatch that defer partition expansion until needed.

nodes.name: WitnessHandle
nodes.summary: Uniform handle abstracting over implicit, eager, and lazy witnesses.

nodes.name: ImplicitWitness
nodes.summary: Minimal witness that stores only the boundary set and a deterministic seed, materialising the partition on demand.

nodes.name: Witness
nodes.summary: Eagerly materialised cut witness holding the full partition and boundary set.

nodes.name: LazyWitness
nodes.summary: Witness whose partition is computed on first access, caching the result for subsequent queries.

nodes.name: LazyWitnessBatch
nodes.summary: Batch container for LazyWitness instances with helpers to materialise the smallest-boundary witness first.

nodes.name: materialize
nodes.summary: Expands a LazyWitness into a full Witness by running deterministic BFS from the seed up to the cached radius.

nodes.name: materialize_partition
nodes.summary: Computes the side-of-cut partition for an ImplicitWitness from its boundary and seed.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Event-driven monitoring layer for the mincut runtime: typed events, threshold crossings, callback registration, and a builder API used by external systems (e.g. the gated transformer's coherence gate) to react to cut changes.

nodes.name: EventType
nodes.summary: Enum covering threshold crossings, cut-value changes, partition updates, and lifecycle events emitted by the monitor.

nodes.name: MinCutEvent
nodes.summary: Payload struct delivered to callbacks: event type, current cut value, threshold, and timestamp.

nodes.name: Threshold
nodes.summary: Bidirectional threshold descriptor (below/above) with crossing detection logic.

nodes.name: MonitorMetrics
nodes.summary: Aggregated counters describing event volume, callback latency, and threshold-crossing history.

nodes.name: MonitorConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for callback fan-out, queue size, and metrics retention used by the monitor.

nodes.name: MinCutMonitor
nodes.summary: Core monitor: holds thresholds, callbacks, and metrics; processes events to fan-out notifications when cut-value changes cross configured boundaries.

nodes.name: MonitorBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder that assembles a MinCutMonitor from thresholds and on-change callbacks.

nodes.name: notify
nodes.summary: Main notification fan-out: updates metrics, checks thresholds, and dispatches matching callbacks for a new cut observation.

nodes.name: update_metrics
nodes.summary: Folds a new event into the rolling metrics counters and updates threshold-crossing history.

nodes.name: causal.rs
nodes.summary: Spiking-neural-network-driven causal-discovery layer for the mincut crate: observes graph events, builds a weighted causal graph with transitive closure, and exposes intervention-point selection used to bias the canonical cut.

nodes.name: CausalConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the SNN causal layer: spike threshold, decay rate, refractory period, and discovery window.

nodes.name: CausalEdge
nodes.summary: Weighted directed edge representing a hypothesised causal influence with decay-tracked strength.

nodes.name: CausalGraph
nodes.summary: Directed weighted graph of causal relations with transitive-closure and undirected-projection helpers.

nodes.name: CausalDiscoverySNN
nodes.summary: Online SNN that observes graph events, accumulates causal edge weights, decays them over time, and proposes optimal intervention points.

nodes.name: transitive_closure
nodes.summary: Computes the reachability matrix of the causal graph used to identify root-cause and downstream nodes.

nodes.name: optimal_intervention_points
nodes.summary: Selects the highest-leverage vertices to intervene on, used to bias the canonical mincut toward causally informed cuts.

nodes.name: summary
nodes.summary: Produces a CausalSummary snapshot describing edge count, strongest relations, and recent activity for monitoring.

nodes.name: time_compat.rs
nodes.summary: Portable instant/timestamp shims that fall back to a wasm-friendly counter when std::time::Instant is unavailable, keeping the mincut crate buildable for both native and wasm targets.

nodes.name: PortableInstant
nodes.summary: Cross-target monotonic timestamp with elapsed/duration helpers, backed by Instant natively and a counter on wasm.

nodes.name: PortableTimestamp
nodes.summary: Wall-clock timestamp wrapper exposing seconds-since-epoch helpers for receipt and event payloads.

nodes.name: test_dynamic_updates_bounded
nodes.summary: Exercises incremental edge insert/delete against the bounded instance and checks invariants.

nodes.summary: End-to-end test that runs community detection over a known graph and validates partition quality.

nodes.name: CITATIONS.bib
nodes.summary: BibTeX citation file backing the gated-transformer documentation with references for FlashAttention, KIVI quantisation, early-exit, and Mixture-of-Depths routing literature.

nodes.name: early_exit.rs
nodes.summary: Coherence-gated early-exit logic for the gated transformer: configurable confidence/entropy thresholds, speculation verification, and an adaptive exit-layer policy driven by mincut coherence signals.

nodes.name: EarlyExitConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for early-exit decisions with aggressive/conservative presets and a validator.

nodes.name: EarlyExitDecision
nodes.summary: Decision record reporting whether to exit, at which layer, and the reason (entropy, confidence, coherence, etc.).

nodes.name: ExitReason
nodes.summary: Enum of reasons the gate decided to exit (or not): confidence, entropy, coherence, max-layer reached, speculation accepted.

nodes.name: CoherenceEarlyExit
nodes.summary: Coherence-driven early-exit engine that combines confidence/entropy with mincut-coherence to decide when to stop layer evaluation, with topk speculation verification.

nodes.name: should_exit
nodes.summary: Top-level decision function combining configured thresholds and the current layer's signals.

nodes.name: evaluate_exit_conditions
nodes.summary: Detailed predicate evaluator over confidence, entropy, and coherence margins producing an EarlyExitDecision.

nodes.name: topk
nodes.summary: Returns the top-k highest-probability indices used by speculation verification.

nodes.name: kivi.rs
nodes.summary: KIVI-style 2-bit per-channel key / per-token value quantiser for the KV cache, with optional Hadamard rotation to flatten outliers and FP16/FP32 compression-ratio helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Quantise/dequantise paths are kept symmetric to make round-trip determinism checks straightforward in tests.

nodes.name: QuantScheme
nodes.summary: Enum selecting per-channel (keys) vs per-token (values) quantisation scheme.

nodes.name: QuantizedKV
nodes.summary: Packed quantised K/V tensor with scale/zero metadata and a compression-ratio helper.

nodes.name: KiviQuantizer
nodes.summary: KIVI quantiser engine with optional Hadamard rotation and key/value-specialised codepaths.

nodes.name: quantize
nodes.summary: Quantises a K or V tensor down to the configured bit width using per-channel or per-token scaling.

nodes.name: dequantize
nodes.summary: Inverse of quantize: reconstructs float values from the packed tensor and stored scale/zero.

nodes.name: hadamard_forward
nodes.summary: Applies a fast Walsh-Hadamard transform that flattens outlier magnitudes before quantisation.

nodes.name: hadamard_inverse
nodes.summary: Inverse Walsh-Hadamard transform paired with hadamard_forward to restore the original representation.

nodes.name: mod_routing.rs
nodes.summary: Mixture-of-Depths router: uses the mincut coherence signal to pick which tokens skip compute at each layer, with per-layer capacity calculation, boundary marking, and stable-vs-unstable routing paths.

nodes.name: ModRoutingConfig
nodes.summary: Routing configuration: target FLOPs reduction, minimum compute floor, and stability thresholds.

nodes.name: TokenRoute
nodes.summary: Per-token routing decision (compute / skip / boundary) annotated with the gate signal that produced it.

nodes.name: MincutDepthRouter
nodes.summary: Router that converts mincut coherence packets into per-layer compute masks, balancing capacity against stability.

nodes.name: RoutingStats
nodes.summary: Statistics about the most recent routing pass: FLOPs ratio, boundary count, skip rate per layer.

nodes.name: route_tokens
nodes.summary: Top-level routing entry point that computes per-layer masks from a coherence packet stream.

nodes.name: routing_stats
nodes.summary: Computes summary statistics over the latest routing decisions used to verify FLOPs reduction targets.

nodes.name: calculate_layer_capacity
nodes.summary: Computes the per-layer compute budget from the configured FLOPs reduction and tokens-in-flight.

nodes.name: mark_boundary_tokens
nodes.summary: Flags tokens that sit on the mincut boundary so they always retain compute regardless of stability.

nodes.name: route_unstable_tokens
nodes.summary: Routing path for tokens whose coherence is unstable: keeps them in compute until they stabilise.

nodes.name: route_stable_tokens
nodes.summary: Routing path for stably-coherent tokens: skips compute at the current layer when capacity is tight.

nodes.name: ensure_minimum_compute
nodes.summary: Floor guard that keeps each layer above a minimum compute fraction to prevent under-utilisation.

nodes.name: determinism_extended.rs
nodes.summary: Extended determinism test suite for the gated transformer: identical inputs produce identical outputs across baseline/micro configs, conservative/permissive policies, all tier configurations, spike inputs, cache hits, and lambda sweeps.

nodes.name: test_determinism_baseline_config
nodes.summary: Determinism check against the baseline configuration: identical inputs yield byte-for-byte identical packets.

nodes.name: test_determinism_micro_config
nodes.summary: Same determinism guarantee on the micro (small-model) configuration.

nodes.name: test_determinism_conservative_policy
nodes.summary: Determinism check with the conservative early-exit policy enabled.

nodes.name: test_determinism_permissive_policy
nodes.summary: Determinism check with the permissive early-exit policy enabled.

nodes.name: test_determinism_across_all_tiers
nodes.summary: Sweeps all tier configurations and verifies determinism on each.

nodes.name: test_determinism_with_spikes
nodes.summary: Determinism with active SNN spike input mixed into the routing signal.

nodes.name: test_determinism_inactive_spikes
nodes.summary: Determinism when SNN spikes are present but inactive (gate-off path).

nodes.name: test_cache_hit_determinism
nodes.summary: Confirms KV-cache hits produce identical outputs to a cold-cache reference run.

nodes.name: test_determinism_lambda_sequences
nodes.summary: Sweeps lambda (penalty) sequences and verifies each produces a reproducible trace.

nodes.name: test_determinism_zero_lambda
nodes.summary: Edge-case test that lambda=0 still produces deterministic output.

nodes.name: test_determinism_max_values
nodes.summary: Edge-case test that maximum-value inputs do not break determinism.

nodes.name: test_stats_reproducibility
nodes.summary: Verifies that routing/early-exit stats are bit-identical across repeated runs.

nodes.name: test_reset_clears_state_deterministically
nodes.summary: Verifies that reset() clears internal state so subsequent runs match a fresh-instance trace.

nodes.name: kv_cache.rs
nodes.summary: KV cache manager for attention layers with quantization (asymmetric/symmetric, configurable bits) and three eviction policies (H2O, sliding-window, PyramidKV) to bound memory while preserving attention quality.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust enum-dispatched eviction policies plus pyramid budget allocation across transformer layers.

nodes.name: EvictionPolicy
nodes.summary: Enum selecting an eviction strategy for the KV cache: H2O (heavy-hitter retention), SlidingWindow (recency-based), or PyramidKV (per-layer budgeting).

nodes.name: KVCacheConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration bundle for cache shape (max_seq_len, num_heads, head_dim), quantization bit width, and chosen eviction policy.

nodes.name: QuantizedTensor
nodes.summary: Storage struct for quantized key/value data with per-group scales, zero-points, and bit width metadata for round-trip dequantization.

nodes.name: CacheManager
nodes.summary: Append-only KV cache with quantized storage, attention-score tracking, and policy-driven eviction; reports compression ratio and memory usage.

nodes.name: quantize_asymmetric
nodes.summary: Per-head asymmetric quantization that computes scale and zero-point per group and packs values to the requested bit width.

nodes.name: quantize_symmetric
nodes.summary: Symmetric quantization that derives a single scale from absolute maxima and encodes signed values around zero.

nodes.name: evict_h2o
nodes.summary: Heavy-Hitter Oracle eviction that retains cache entries with highest accumulated attention scores under a budget.

nodes.name: evict_sliding_window
nodes.summary: Sliding-window eviction keeping the most recent window plus a small number of attention sink tokens at the sequence start.

nodes.name: pyramid_budget
nodes.summary: Computes PyramidKV per-layer cache budget that tapers from the base layer to higher layers for memory-efficient long-context inference.

nodes.name: error.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the AttentionError enum covering dimension mismatches, invalid configurations, computation failures, memory issues, head-count errors, and empty inputs for the attention crate.

nodes.name: AttentionError
nodes.summary: Crate-wide error enum for attention computations using thiserror, with variants for shape, config, compute, memory, and input failure modes.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel re-exporting the information-bottleneck submodules (bottleneck objective and KL divergence helpers) used as the information-theoretic regularizer for the mincut-attention bridge.

nodes.name: builder.rs
nodes.summary: Fluent SDK builder that selects an attention variant (scaled-dot, multi-head, flash, linear, local-global, hyperbolic) and configures dropout, causality, and head counts before constructing an attention instance.

nodes.name: AttentionType
nodes.summary: Enum of the six attention variants exposed by the SDK builder: ScaledDot, MultiHead, Flash, Linear, LocalGlobal, and Hyperbolic.

nodes.name: AttentionBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder that accumulates attention configuration (dim, type, dropout, causal, heads) before producing the configured attention object.

nodes.name: local_global.rs
nodes.summary: Sparse local-global attention combining a sliding local window with a handful of always-attended global tokens for sub-quadratic long-context compute.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the LocalGlobalAttention pattern used by Longformer/BigBird-style models.

nodes.name: LocalGlobalAttention
nodes.summary: Sparse attention module computing local-window scores plus scores against a small set of global tokens, with optional mask support.

nodes.name: compute
nodes.summary: Runs the local-global attention forward pass: per-position local-window softmax merged with global-token scores to produce the output tensor.

nodes.name: traits.rs
nodes.summary: Core trait surface for the attention crate, defining Attention, GraphAttention, GeometricAttention, SparseAttention, and TrainableAttention interfaces plus supporting structs (SparseMask, EdgeInfo, Gradients).
nodes.languageNotes: Trait hierarchy lets the mincut and gated-transformer crates plug different attention variants behind a common interface.

nodes.name: Attention
nodes.summary: Base trait for any attention implementation, exposing compute, masked compute, dim, and num_heads.

nodes.name: GraphAttention
nodes.summary: Trait for attention that incorporates graph edge information (edges-aware compute and per-edge attention).

nodes.name: GeometricAttention
nodes.summary: Trait for attention operating in non-Euclidean geometry (hyperbolic/spherical) with explicit project-to and project-from methods.

nodes.name: SparseAttention
nodes.summary: Trait for sparse attention variants that operate against a SparseMask, with a mask-generation hook.

nodes.name: TrainableAttention
nodes.summary: Trait for attention modules supporting forward/backward passes and parameter updates, enabling end-to-end gradient training.

nodes.name: gating.rs
nodes.summary: Implements mincut-gated attention: standard softmax attention plus a variant that blends the softmax distribution with a mincut-derived gating signal controlled by lambda/tau/eps.
nodes.languageNotes: Core algorithmic bridge between attention scores and mincut partition signals — the heart of slice 4.

nodes.name: attn_softmax
nodes.summary: Reference softmax-attention pipeline (logits, row softmax, weighted value sum) used as the baseline against mincut gating.

nodes.name: attn_mincut
nodes.summary: Mincut-gated attention that fuses softmax attention with a partition-aware gating distribution using lambda mixing weight, temperature tau, and stability eps.

nodes.name: AttentionOutput
nodes.summary: Output struct carrying both the attention output tensor and the gating signal used to produce it.

nodes.name: ewc.rs
nodes.summary: Elastic Weight Consolidation implementation for continual learning: tracks per-parameter Fisher information and anchor weights, applies a quadratic penalty (and gradient) to prevent catastrophic forgetting in the GNN.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements Kirkpatrick et al. 2017 EWC; consumed by the gnn training loop that feeds the mincut/attention pipeline.

nodes.name: ElasticWeightConsolidation
nodes.summary: EWC state holding Fisher diagonal, anchor weights, regularization strength lambda, and an active flag; exposes compute_fisher, consolidate, penalty, gradient, and reset.

nodes.name: compute_fisher
nodes.summary: Estimates the Fisher information diagonal from accumulated squared gradients averaged across samples.

nodes.name: penalty
nodes.summary: Quadratic EWC penalty (lambda/2) * sum F_i * (theta_i - theta_i*)^2 against the stored anchor weights.

nodes.name: gradient
nodes.summary: Analytic gradient of the EWC penalty term used as an extra additive gradient during continual training.

nodes.name: loss_verification.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests verifying that the GNN's loss implementations (MSE, cross-entropy, binary cross-entropy) and their gradients are numerically correct and shape-consistent.

nodes.summary: In-depth algorithm documentation for ruvector-mincut covering dynamic minimum cut, link-cut trees, Euler tour trees, hierarchical decomposition, graph sparsification, and complexity analysis with mathematical foundations and pseudocode.

nodes.summary: Summary of the bounded testing strategy and results for the mincut crate, characterizing coverage, performance envelopes, and verification methodology.

nodes.summary: Single benchmark result row: baseline/optimized timings, computed speedup, target threshold, and pass/fail status with optional memory metric.

nodes.name: cognitive_engine.rs

nodes.name: EngineConfig
nodes.summary: Feature-flag config enabling or disabling the six cognitive subsystems (attractors, strange-loop, causal discovery, time-crystal, morphogenetic, optimizer).

nodes.name: EngineMetrics
nodes.summary: Telemetry struct accumulating per-subsystem timings (attractor, strange-loop, causal, CPG, morph) plus total time.

nodes.name: OperationMode
nodes.summary: Selectable operation modes for the cognitive engine: Optimize, CausalDiscovery, Attractor, Crystal, MetaCognitive, Morphogenetic.

nodes.name: CognitiveMinCutEngine
nodes.summary: Top-level engine binding a graph with attractor, strange-loop, causal, time-crystal, morphogenetic, and optimizer components; orchestrates stepping, convergence, and search.

nodes.name: EngineSummary
nodes.summary: Diagnostic snapshot of engine state: current mode, elapsed time, graph size, current mincut value, and synchrony measure.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Hierarchical balanced-binary decomposition for dynamic min-cut queries: builds a leaf-per-vertex tree, supports incremental insert/delete with lazy recomputation, and exposes min-cut value, partition, and per-level info.

nodes.name: DecompositionNode
nodes.summary: Internal/leaf node in the hierarchical decomposition tree carrying vertex set, parent/children indices, and cached cut value.

nodes.name: HierarchicalDecomposition
nodes.summary: Hierarchical decomposition data structure supporting build, incremental edge insert/delete, lazy propagation of updates, LCA queries, and dynamic min-cut queries.

nodes.name: LevelInfo
nodes.summary: Per-level summary: level index, number of nodes, and average cut value.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Constructs a fresh hierarchical decomposition from a graph, building leaves bottom-up and computing initial cut values.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Incrementally inserts an edge into the underlying graph and marks affected ancestors dirty for lazy recomputation.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Incrementally deletes an edge and marks affected decomposition ancestors dirty.

nodes.name: propagate_updates
nodes.summary: Walks dirty subtrees and recomputes their cut values, propagating new minimums up the decomposition.

nodes.name: lca_node
nodes.summary: Computes the lowest common ancestor of two vertices in the decomposition tree, used to localize update propagation.

nodes.summary: Theoretical foundations of the mincut-gated transformer: coherence-gated inference, Mixture-of-Depths routing, tier-based execution, and the link between graph-partition metrics and adaptive compute.

nodes.name: energy_gate.rs
nodes.summary: Energy gate that scores a gate signal via a weighted combination of lambda, boundary, and partition-entropy terms, computes gradients, refines decisions, and chooses interventions (reduce/flush/freeze) with confidence and fallback policies.
nodes.languageNotes: Converts the mincut signal into a discrete intervention tier — the runtime hinge of the gated-transformer.

nodes.name: EnergyGateConfig
nodes.summary: Weights and thresholds for the energy gate: lambda weight, boundary penalty, partition entropy weight, convexity radius, gradient steps, and intervention threshold.

nodes.name: EnergyGradient
nodes.summary: Gradient of the energy function with components along lambda, boundary, and partition axes plus a magnitude helper.

nodes.name: EnergyGate
nodes.summary: Stateful energy gate combining config, fallback policy, and methods to compute energy, gradients, refine the decision via local search, and select an intervention tier.

nodes.name: compute_energy
nodes.summary: Computes the scalar energy of a gate signal as a weighted sum of lambda term, boundary penalty, and partition entropy.

nodes.name: energy_gradient
nodes.summary: Computes the gradient of the energy function with respect to lambda, boundary, and partition terms.

nodes.name: decide
nodes.summary: Top-level decision: evaluates energy, picks an intervention tier (reduce/flush/freeze/quarantine), and tags the result with a confidence score.

nodes.name: refine_decision
nodes.summary: Iteratively refines a gate decision via bounded gradient steps inside the convexity radius before committing to an intervention.

nodes.name: determine_intervention
nodes.summary: Maps energy and gradient magnitude to a concrete intervention class (reduce-scope, flush-cache, freeze-writes, quarantine).

nodes.name: kvquant.rs
nodes.summary: KVQuant-style KV cache quantizer with pre-/post-RoPE key modes and uniform/non-uniform value modes; supports calibration data, per-head outlier handling, and RoPE-aware dequantization for low-bit caches.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements pre-RoPE key quantization with outlier-aware non-uniform value quant, citing KVQuant (Hooper et al., 2024).

nodes.name: KVQuantKeyMode
nodes.summary: Enum selecting whether keys are quantized before or after RoPE is applied.

nodes.name: KVQuantValueMode
nodes.summary: Enum selecting uniform vs non-uniform (outlier-aware) value quantization.

nodes.name: PreRoPEKey
nodes.summary: Storage struct for a pre-RoPE quantized key: data, scale, zero-point, position, and dim.

nodes.name: QuantizedValue
nodes.summary: Quantized value tensor with optional outlier indices/values preserved at full precision for non-uniform modes.

nodes.name: CalibrationData
nodes.summary: Per-channel calibration statistics and clip ranges for keys and values used to tune the quantizer.

nodes.name: KVQuantQuantizer

nodes.name: quantize_key_pre_rope
nodes.summary: Quantizes a key vector before RoPE is applied, recording the position so dequantization can re-apply RoPE.

nodes.name: quantize_value_nonuniform
nodes.summary: Non-uniform value quantization that keeps top-percentile outliers at full precision and uniformly quantizes the remainder.

nodes.name: dequantize_key_with_rope
nodes.summary: Dequantizes a pre-RoPE key and applies RoPE at the stored position to produce the runtime key vector.

nodes.name: calibrate
nodes.summary: Computes per-channel calibration statistics and clip ranges from key/value sample tensors.

nodes.summary: Quantized linear layer storing packed weights, per-channel scale/zero, and bias; provides shape validation and weight extraction.

nodes.name: TransformerLayerWeights

nodes.summary: Main inference path: scores cheap input features, consults the energy gate for a tier, optionally short-circuits on early exit, and runs the per-tier layer schedule.

nodes.summary: Executes the transformer block stack for a given tier, honoring mod-routing decisions and per-tier compute budgets.

nodes.name: fisher_rao_distance

nodes.summary: Advances one development step: diffuses chemicals, fires neurons, and triggers growth-rule updates.

nodes.name: apply_growth_rules
nodes.summary: Applies the configured growth rules to add or remove synaptic connections based on local chemical fields.

nodes.name: canonical_init
nodes.summary: Initialises a canonical min-cut session and returns a handle the JS side can keep reusing.

nodes.name: canonical_compute
nodes.summary: Runs canonical min-cut computation on the WASM-side session and stores the partition/hash for retrieval.

nodes.name: dynamic_init
nodes.summary: Initialises a dynamic min-cut session that supports incremental edge insertion/removal in WASM.

nodes.name: dynamic_compute
nodes.summary: Recomputes dynamic min-cut after batched edge updates and updates the epoch counter for JS observers.

nodes.name: FfnConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the quantized FFN specifying hidden width, activation kind, and dropout.

nodes.name: QuantizedFfn
nodes.summary: Quantized two-layer feed-forward block with i8 weights and i32 accumulators, used in the min-cut gated transformer.

nodes.name: forward
nodes.summary: Forward pass through the quantized FFN: matmul, activation, dropout, and dequantise to f32 outputs.

nodes.name: apply_gelu_simd
nodes.summary: AVX2-optimised GELU activation that processes a slice of f32 elements per vector instruction.

nodes.name: apply_gelu_neon
nodes.summary: NEON-optimised GELU activation for ARM/Apple Silicon targets.

nodes.name: apply_activation_i32_to_f32
nodes.summary: Applies the configured activation to i32 accumulator output and dequantises to f32 with scale handling.

nodes.name: AdaptiveKVCacheConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the adaptive KV cache including tier thresholds and memory budgets, with presets for small/large/extreme contexts.

nodes.name: AdaptiveKVCache
nodes.summary: Multi-tier adaptive KV cache that compresses older tokens via archive quantization and exposes attention/append APIs to the gated transformer.

nodes.name: append
nodes.summary: Appends new K/V tensors to the current tier, evicting and quantising to lower tiers when thresholds are exceeded.

nodes.name: attention
nodes.summary: Computes attention over the multi-tier KV cache, fusing precision-aware dot products across tiers and applying min-cut gating.

nodes.name: adapt_thresholds
nodes.summary: Adapts tier-transition thresholds based on recent quality metrics so the cache trades memory for quality dynamically.

nodes.name: Q15
nodes.summary: Q15 fixed-point scalar type with 1 sign + 15 fractional bits, saturating arithmetic, and f32 conversion helpers.

nodes.name: lerp
nodes.summary: Linear interpolation between two Q15 values using saturating arithmetic to avoid overflow.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Demo entrypoint that constructs Q/K/V tensors and runs the FlashAttention v3 path of the gated transformer end-to-end.

nodes.name: multi_head.rs
nodes.summary: Multi-head attention building block in the ruvector-attention crate — splits input into parallel attention heads, applies scaled dot-product attention to each, then concatenates the results to drive the slice-4 attention stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust struct + impl pattern; delegates per-head work to ScaledDotProductAttention from a sibling module.

nodes.name: MultiHeadAttention
nodes.summary: Multi-head attention mechanism storing embedding dim, head count, and head_dim; orchestrates split/attend/concat across heads to feed the rest of the attention stack.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a MultiHeadAttention; panics if `dim` is not divisible by `num_heads` so head_dim is well defined.

nodes.name: compute
nodes.summary: Core multi-head forward pass: splits Q/K/V into heads, applies scaled-dot-product attention per head, and concatenates outputs back to the model dimension.

nodes.name: compute_with_mask
nodes.summary: Masked variant of the multi-head forward pass — accepts an attention mask and delegates to the per-head scaled-dot-product attention with masking.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the graph subsystem in ruvector-attention — re-exports dual-space, edge-featured graph, and rotary positional embedding helpers used by graph-aware attention.

nodes.name: natural_gradient.rs
nodes.summary: Natural gradient descent optimizer for the attention stack — uses the Fisher information matrix to precondition gradients, stabilising training of softmax logits and parameter blocks.
nodes.languageNotes: Pairs a config struct with the optimizer; the diagonal Fisher path is the fast default.

nodes.name: NaturalGradientConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration record for the natural-gradient optimizer: learning rate, Fisher metric config, and a toggle for the cheap diagonal approximation.

nodes.name: NaturalGradient
nodes.summary: Natural-gradient optimizer wrapping a FisherMetric — applies F⁻¹·grad updates either to attention logits or to generic parameter vectors.

nodes.name: step_logits
nodes.summary: Single natural-gradient step over softmax logits — preconditions the gradient with the Fisher metric specialised for categorical distributions.

nodes.name: step_diagonal
nodes.summary: Cheap diagonal natural-gradient update: scales each parameter gradient by the inverse of its diagonal Fisher value plus damping.

nodes.name: softmax
nodes.summary: Numerically stable softmax helper used to convert logits into probability vectors before the Fisher computation.

nodes.name: presets.rs
nodes.summary: Ergonomic AttentionPreset enum for the public SDK — turns named architectures (Bert, Gpt, Longformer, Flash-optimized, etc.) into pre-configured AttentionBuilder instances.

nodes.name: AttentionPreset
nodes.summary: Enum of common attention configurations exposed in the SDK; each variant yields a tuned AttentionBuilder via `builder()`.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Converts an AttentionPreset variant into a populated AttentionBuilder — currently specialises Bert/Gpt and falls back to defaults for others.

nodes.name: coherence.rs
nodes.summary: Window coherence metrics for attention — computes structural permission signals (boundary mass, cut proxy, neighbor disagreement, similarity variance) over a k-NN graph of keys to decide when an attention window is fragmented vs coherent.
nodes.languageNotes: Stateful WindowCoherence keeps staleness/tick counters so coherence can be recomputed periodically rather than every token.

nodes.name: CoherenceMetric
nodes.summary: Enum identifying which structural coherence signal to compute — BoundaryMass, CutProxy, Disagreement, or SimilarityVariance.

nodes.name: WindowCoherence
nodes.summary: Per-window coherence state holding an overall fragment-vs-coherent score, individual metric values, and tokens-since-update bookkeeping for stale detection.

nodes.name: compute
nodes.summary: Builds a k-NN graph over the window's keys and evaluates the requested coherence metrics, producing a fresh WindowCoherence with score and per-metric values.

nodes.name: build_knn_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a k-nearest-neighbour graph over the supplied keys using squared distance — the structural backbone for all coherence metrics.

nodes.name: boundary_mass
nodes.summary: Computes the boundary-mass coherence signal: ratio of edges crossing the window boundary to total k-NN edges.

nodes.name: cut_proxy
nodes.summary: Cheap cut-proxy estimate over the k-NN graph — surrogate for an exact min-cut on the attention window.

nodes.name: disagreement
nodes.summary: Disagreement coherence metric measuring how much neighbouring labels diverge in the k-NN window.

nodes.name: similarity_variance
nodes.summary: Computes the variance of cosine similarities across neighbour pairs as a smooth coherence signal.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module root for the optimal-transport subsystem of ruvector-attention — re-exports sliced Wasserstein, centroid OT, and cached projection routines used to compute transport-based attention scores.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for ruvector-attn-mincut — replaces softmax attention with dynamic min-cut gating: builds a graph from Q·Kᵀ logits, runs Dinic's max-flow to cut weak edges, then row-softmaxes the survivors against V.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Rust public-API re-export hub; concrete algorithms live in sibling modules (graph, mincut, gating, hysteresis, witness).

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for ruvector-gnn — graph-neural-network capabilities (layers, training, EWC, replay, GraphMAE, schedulers, search, compression) that feed embeddings and learned topology into the attention/mincut stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Conditionally exposes mmap and cold-tier features; doc comment shows Adam/Replay/EWC/Scheduler bootstrap pattern.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Benchmark suite documentation for ruvector-mincut — describes how to run criterion benchmarks for dynamic graph edge ops, connectivity queries, mixed workloads, and the subpolynomial O(n^{2/3}) scaling verification.

nodes.name: BENCHMARK_REPORT.md
nodes.summary: Captured performance report for ruvector-mincut 0.2.0 — quantifies DynamicMinCut, PolylogConnectivity, ApproxMinCut, and CacheOptBFS timings against 100–10k-vertex graphs and notes complexity claims from the 2025 papers.

nodes.name: 03-practical-applications.md
nodes.summary: Practical applications guide for ruvector-mincut — walks through network-security, social-network, image-segmentation, supply-chain, and load-balancing use cases with complete code examples that motivate the slice-4 mincut algorithms.

nodes.name: sparsify_demo.rs
nodes.summary: Runnable example that contrasts three graph-sparsification algorithms (Benczúr-Karger, plain Karger, Nagamochi-Ibaraki) against the same demo graph — shows the mincut crate's public sparsifier APIs end-to-end.

nodes.summary: Entry point for the sparsify demo binary — constructs a small weighted graph and runs each of the three sparsifier algorithms in turn.

nodes.name: demo_benczur_karger
nodes.summary: Demonstrates the Benczúr-Karger cut sparsifier on the demo graph, printing the resulting sparse graph stats.

nodes.name: demo_karger
nodes.summary: Demonstrates basic Karger random-contraction sparsification on the demo graph.

nodes.name: demo_nagamochi_ibaraki
nodes.summary: Demonstrates Nagamochi-Ibaraki forest-packing sparsification on the demo graph.

nodes.name: tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit-test module for the canonical tree-packing/Gomory-Hu implementation — verifies triangle/path/two-cluster Gomory-Hu correctness, Stoer-Wagner agreement, and deterministic results across 100 reruns.

nodes.summary: Asserts Gomory-Hu produces the expected cut values for the canonical triangle test graph.

nodes.name: test_tree_global_mincut_matches_stoer_wagner
nodes.summary: Cross-checks that the tree-packing global min-cut matches the reference Stoer-Wagner result on several graphs.

nodes.summary: Confirms the fast canonical decomposition agrees with the tier-1 Gomory-Hu tree on shared cut queries.

nodes.name: test_gomory_hu_deterministic_100_runs
nodes.summary: Runs the Gomory-Hu construction 100 times and asserts the result is bit-identical — guards against nondeterminism regressions.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Deterministic expander-decomposition core for dynamic min-cut — partitions a graph into φ-expanders (Chuzhoy / Saranurak-Wang style), maintains the O(log n)-level hierarchy under edge insert/delete, and exposes conductance queries that the rest of the slice-4 mincut stack depends on.
nodes.languageNotes: Public API surface is rich (11 exports); each level rebuild is gated by a φ threshold and a connected-components check.

nodes.name: ExpanderComponent
nodes.summary: A single high-expansion component in the decomposition — stores id, vertex set, level, and offers contains/size accessors.

nodes.name: ExpanderDecomposition
nodes.summary: Top-level expander-decomposition struct: builds the multi-level hierarchy, answers `component_at_level`, and absorbs dynamic edge insertions/deletions to keep the decomposition valid.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Constructs the initial expander decomposition for a given graph and conductance threshold φ, populating all O(log n) levels.

nodes.name: component_at_level
nodes.summary: Returns the component containing vertex v at the specified level of the hierarchy.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Handles dynamic insertion of an edge — updates affected hierarchy levels while preserving the φ-expander invariant.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Handles dynamic deletion of an edge, restructuring affected levels and triggering re-decomposition where conductance falls below φ.

nodes.name: build_hierarchy
nodes.summary: Builds the O(log n)-level expander hierarchy from a flat decomposition by recursive trimming and partitioning.

nodes.name: compute_conductance
nodes.summary: Computes the conductance φ(S) = cut(S, V\S) / min(vol(S), vol(V\S)) for a candidate vertex set.

nodes.name: local_cut_search
nodes.summary: Local cut search from a start vertex — explores expanding boundaries until a target-conductance cut is found or no further progress is possible.

nodes.name: find_connected_components
nodes.summary: Identifies connected components within a vertex subset by BFS, used to validate decomposition invariants.

nodes.name: estimate_conductance
nodes.summary: Cheap conductance estimate over a vertex subset — accelerates trimming decisions during hierarchy construction.

nodes.name: deterministic_decompose
nodes.summary: Deterministic decomposition step that splits a vertex set into a φ-expander plus residual along the lowest-conductance cut.

nodes.name: bfs_local_search
nodes.summary: BFS-based variant of the local cut search — bounded expansion from the start vertex until target-φ cut is detected.

nodes.name: level.rs
nodes.summary: Single level of the j-tree hierarchy for dynamic min-cut — wraps a contracted graph, optionally backed by BMSSP WASM shortest-path queries, to answer min_cut/multi_terminal_cut/refine_cut via path-cut duality at level-specific approximation α.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines a native Rust Dijkstra-style path solver with an optional WASM-backed BMSSP shortest-path engine plus an LRU-style path cache.

nodes.name: LevelConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for a single j-tree level: index, α approximation factor, cache toggle and bounds.

nodes.name: ContractedGraph
nodes.summary: Contracted graph representation for a j-tree level — maps original vertices to super-vertices and tracks aggregated edge weights.

nodes.name: JTreeLevel
nodes.summary: Core j-tree level abstraction exposing min_cut/multi_terminal_cut/refine_cut over the contracted graph; refines coarse cuts produced by higher levels.

nodes.name: BmsspJTreeLevel
nodes.summary: BMSSP-backed j-tree level that delegates shortest-path queries to the WASM BMSSP solver — exploits path-cut duality to get O(m·log^{2/3} n) cut queries.

nodes.name: WasmGraphHandle
nodes.summary: FFI handle to the WASM-resident graph used by BMSSP shortest-path queries.

nodes.name: from_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a ContractedGraph from a base DynamicGraph at a given level, copying vertices and edges into the contracted representation.

nodes.name: contract
nodes.summary: Contracts two vertices u and v into a single super-vertex, merging incident edges and adjusting weights.

nodes.name: native_shortest_paths
nodes.summary: Pure-Rust shortest-path solver used as a fallback when BMSSP WASM is unavailable — drives the dual-graph cut queries.

nodes.name: min_cut
nodes.summary: Answers a single-pair s-t min-cut query at this level by translating to a shortest-path query in the dual graph.

nodes.name: multi_terminal_cut
nodes.summary: Computes a multi-terminal cut value across the supplied terminal vertices at this level.

nodes.name: refine_cut
nodes.summary: Refines a coarse cut handed down from a higher j-tree level into a cut over the current finer contracted graph.

nodes.name: insert_edge
nodes.summary: Reflects an inserted edge into the contracted graph and invalidates affected cache entries.

nodes.name: delete_edge
nodes.summary: Reflects a deleted edge into the contracted graph and invalidates affected cache entries.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel module aggregating the mincut crate's optimization helpers — benchmarks, distance caches, dSpar sparsifier, parallel runners, memory pools, SIMD distance kernels, and WASM batching glue.

nodes.name: network.rs
nodes.summary: Spiking neural network architecture for the mincut crate — layered LIF networks (feedforward / recurrent / graph-coupled) that mirror dynamic-graph topology, run step-wise spike simulation, and feed synchrony signals back into the mincut decision layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses rayon for layer-parallel updates; graph-coupled mode wires synapse matrices directly from the dynamic graph topology.

nodes.name: LayerConfig
nodes.summary: Single-layer SNN config: neuron count, neuron parameters, and whether lateral recurrent connections are enabled.

nodes.name: NetworkConfig
nodes.summary: Full spiking-network configuration: layer stack, recurrence flags, STDP parameters, and integration cadence.

nodes.name: SpikingNetwork
nodes.summary: Multi-layer spiking network that runs LIF dynamics, supports graph-coupled topology, exposes per-layer rates and global synchrony, and accepts reward signals for online plasticity.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a SpikingNetwork from a NetworkConfig, allocating populations and synapse matrices per layer.

nodes.name: from_graph
nodes.summary: Builds a graph-coupled spiking network whose layer topology mirrors a DynamicGraph — the bridge between mincut graphs and spiking computation.

nodes.name: step
nodes.summary: Advances the spiking network one simulation tick — propagates currents, integrates LIF dynamics, applies STDP, and records spikes.

nodes.name: run_until_decision
nodes.summary: Runs the network forward up to max_steps or until a stable decision is reached.

nodes.name: global_synchrony
nodes.summary: Computes a global synchrony scalar across all layers — used by the mincut decision layer.

nodes.name: synchrony_matrix
nodes.summary: Computes a pairwise per-layer synchrony matrix from recent spike trains.

nodes.name: apply_reward
nodes.summary: Applies a reinforcement reward signal to the synaptic weights for online learning.

nodes.name: low_activity_regions
nodes.summary: Identifies layer regions whose firing rate falls below threshold — surfaces candidate cut regions.

nodes.name: sync_to_graph
nodes.summary: Synchronises learned spiking-network connectivity back to the source DynamicGraph.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: WASM-facing barrel for the mincut crate — re-exports the agentic, canonical, and SIMD WASM bindings used when ruvector-mincut is compiled for the browser/edge.

nodes.name: paper_algorithm_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Integration test suite validating the published min-cut algorithms inside ruvector-mincut — covers LocalKCut, forest packing, fragmentation, trimming, hierarchy invariants, mirror-cut certificates, and brute-force ground truth.

nodes.name: brute_force_min_cut
nodes.summary: Brute-force ground-truth min-cut for tiny graphs — used as the oracle against which the published algorithms are checked.

nodes.name: is_connected
nodes.summary: Helper that decides whether a subset of vertices forms a connected subgraph; used to validate decomposition outputs.

nodes.name: test_localkcut_finds_small_cuts
nodes.summary: Asserts LocalKCut finds small cuts that match the expected paper guarantees on a crafted graph.

nodes.name: test_localkcut_respects_volume_bound
nodes.summary: Checks that LocalKCut never explores beyond its theoretical volume bound.

nodes.name: test_forest_packing_no_cycles
nodes.summary: Validates the forest-packing decomposition produces cycle-free spanning forests.

nodes.name: test_fragmentation_covers_all_vertices
nodes.summary: Asserts the fragmentation step partitions every vertex exactly once.

nodes.name: test_hierarchy_levels_consistent
nodes.summary: Cross-checks that hierarchy levels remain internally consistent across edge updates.

nodes.name: test_hierarchy_global_min_cut_bound
nodes.summary: Confirms the hierarchy's claimed global min-cut bound matches the brute-force ground truth.

nodes.name: test_mirror_cuts_between_expanders
nodes.summary: Validates the mirror-cut construction between sibling expander components.

nodes.summary: Asserts the mirror-cut certificate verifies as a valid global cut.

nodes.name: test_brute_force_matches_known_cut
nodes.summary: Smoke check that the brute-force oracle itself agrees with hand-computed cuts on tiny graphs.

nodes.name: mamba_example.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end example exercising the mincut-gated transformer with a Mamba-style configuration — sets up the gate, KV cache, and runs a sample forward pass to demonstrate the slice-4 transformer integration.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Single end-to-end demo: configures the mincut-gated transformer in Mamba mode, runs the gate + KV cache + attention pipeline, and prints diagnostics.

nodes.name: flash_attention.rs
nodes.summary: FlashAttention-style tiled CPU attention for the mincut-gated transformer — block-wise Q/K/V tiling, online softmax, fused tiled GEMM, optional INT8 quantisation, plus multi-head and naive reference paths used to validate correctness in the slice-4 stack.
nodes.languageNotes: no_std-friendly (extern alloc); INT8 path mirrors the f32 path so accuracy can be compared directly.

nodes.name: FlashAttentionConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration struct controlling block sizes, head dim, causal masking, and softmax scale for FlashAttention.

nodes.name: OnlineSoftmaxState
nodes.summary: Streaming online-softmax accumulator that fuses running max, denominator, and weighted output to avoid materialising the full attention matrix.

nodes.name: for_head_dim
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a sensible FlashAttentionConfig tuned for a given head dimension.

nodes.name: for_long_sequence
nodes.summary: Factory returning a FlashAttentionConfig with larger tiles tuned for long-sequence workloads.

nodes.name: update
nodes.summary: Incrementally updates the online-softmax state with a new score/value tile — preserves running max + denominator invariants.

nodes.name: tile_gemm_qk
nodes.summary: Fused tiled GEMM that multiplies a Q tile by a transposed K tile and writes scaled attention scores into the scores tile.

nodes.name: apply_causal_mask
nodes.summary: Applies a causal mask to a scores tile so future tokens are excluded from attention.

nodes.name: flash_attention_forward
nodes.summary: Main FlashAttention forward pass over f32 inputs — iterates Q/K/V tiles, runs fused GEMM + online softmax, and writes the output.

nodes.name: flash_attention_forward_i8
nodes.summary: INT8 FlashAttention forward path with per-tensor scales — same tiling/online-softmax structure as the f32 version but on quantised inputs.

nodes.name: tile_gemm_qk_i8
nodes.summary: INT8 counterpart of `tile_gemm_qk` — fused tiled Q·Kᵀ in INT8 with per-tensor scales.

nodes.name: flash_mha
nodes.summary: Multi-head wrapper around `flash_attention_forward` — strides through heads and calls the single-head forward per head.

nodes.name: naive_attention
nodes.summary: Reference (unfused, O(n²) memory) attention implementation used to validate FlashAttention correctness in tests.

nodes.name: metrics.rs
nodes.summary: Quality-tracking and adaptive-threshold metrics for the mincut-gated transformer's tiered KV cache — records perplexity/accuracy feedback per tier, tracks memory savings, and decides when the boundary between hot/warm/cold cache should adapt.
nodes.languageNotes: no_std-friendly via the `no_std_gateway` feature; uses a VecDeque-backed rolling history.

nodes.name: MemoryStats
nodes.summary: Memory-usage breakdown across hot/warm/cold KV cache tiers, with helpers for percentages and absolute memory saved.

nodes.name: QualityFeedback
nodes.summary: Single quality feedback record (PPL or accuracy) carrying timestamp and worst-tier annotation.

nodes.name: QualityMetric
nodes.summary: Aggregated quality metric with stability/improvement checks and target-comparison helpers.

nodes.name: TierMetrics
nodes.summary: Per-tier metric snapshot (hits, evictions, quality contribution) for the tiered KV cache.

nodes.name: QualityTracker
nodes.summary: Stateful tracker that ingests QualityFeedback samples, computes trends and tier-level metrics, and recommends whether the tier-boundary thresholds should adapt.

nodes.name: from_ppl
nodes.summary: Builds a QualityFeedback from a measured perplexity relative to a baseline.

nodes.name: record
nodes.summary: Records one QualityFeedback sample into the rolling history and updates internal trend state.

nodes.name: current_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes the current aggregated quality metric across the recent history window.

nodes.name: compute_trend
nodes.summary: Computes the recent quality trend (improving/stable/regressing) from the history buffer.

nodes.name: tier_metrics
nodes.summary: Returns per-tier metric breakdown from the recent feedback history.

nodes.name: should_adapt
nodes.summary: Returns whether the current trend/quality state warrants re-tuning the tier-boundary thresholds.

nodes.name: boundary_adjustment_factor
nodes.summary: Computes a scalar adjustment factor for the hot/warm/cold tier boundary based on current trend.

nodes.name: rope.rs
nodes.summary: Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) for the mincut-gated transformer — precomputes cos/sin tables and applies positional rotation to Q/K vectors, with NTK-aware and YaRN scaling strategies for long-context extension.
nodes.languageNotes: no_std-friendly; provides both f32 and Q1.15 fixed-point apply paths for INT8 deployments.

nodes.name: RopeConfig
nodes.summary: RoPE configuration: head dim, base frequency, max precomputed sequence length, and the scaling strategy.

nodes.name: RopeScaling
nodes.summary: Enum of RoPE scaling strategies — None, Linear, NTK-Aware, and YaRN — selecting how positional frequencies stretch for long contexts.

nodes.name: RopeEmbedding
nodes.summary: Precomputed RoPE embedding tables (cos/sin per position and frequency pair) plus apply-rotation methods for f32 and Q1.15 inputs.

nodes.name: new
nodes.summary: Constructs a RopeEmbedding from a RopeConfig — derives effective base frequency, computes per-position cos/sin tables, and stores them for fast lookup.

nodes.name: compute_effective_base
nodes.summary: Derives the effective base frequency after applying the chosen RopeScaling strategy.

nodes.name: compute_frequencies
nodes.summary: Computes the per-dimension-pair rotation frequencies given a base and scaling strategy.

nodes.name: apply_rotary_pos_emb
nodes.summary: Applies the precomputed cos/sin tables to rotate Q and K vectors in place for f32 inputs.

nodes.name: apply_rotary_pos_emb_q15
nodes.summary: Applies RoPE rotation to Q/K vectors stored in Q1.15 fixed-point — used when the transformer runs in INT8/Q1.15 quantised mode.

nodes.name: get_cos
nodes.summary: Accessor returning the cos table entry for a given position and dimension pair.

nodes.name: get_sin
nodes.summary: Accessor returning the sin table entry for a given position and dimension pair.

layers.name: Slice 4: Attention - Geometric & Manifold
layers.description: ruvector-attention geometric variants: sheaf attention/restriction maps, hyperbolic/Poincare/Lorentz/mixed-curvature, sliced-Wasserstein optimal transport, curvature-aware fused attention, info-bottleneck and info-geometry (Fisher, natural gradient), and PDE-driven diffusion/Laplacian attention.

layers.name: Slice 4: Attention - Flash, Sparse & KV Cache
layers.description: ruvector-attention transformer baseline: scaled dot-product, multi-head, FlashAttention, Mamba SSM, MLA latent-KV, KV cache, plus the sparse-attention family (flash sparse, linear, local-global, masks) and graph-positional features (RoPE, dual-space, edge-featured).

layers.name: Slice 4: Attention - Routing, MoE & Topology Gating
layers.description: ruvector-attention dynamic routing surface: Mixture-of-Experts (router, experts, MoE-attention), speculative decoding, topology-gated attention with coherence and policy modules, and the unified-report metrics for cross-variant comparison.

layers.name: Slice 4: Attention - Training, SDK & Crate Entry
layers.description: ruvector-attention training stack (curriculum, mining, loss, optimizer), public SDK (builder, pipeline, presets), crate root/lib.rs, traits, config, error, utils, plus its examples and benches.

layers.name: Slice 4: Attn-Mincut Bridge Operator
