tour.title: Slice 4 Orientation: Attention/Mincut Stack
tour.description: Slice 4 is the algorithm layer that sits between slice-3 graph/math primitives and downstream agents. Its central insight is that softmax — the attention bottleneck of modern transformers — can be REPLACED by a dynamic graph min-cut, gated by a hysteresis policy. These three READMEs introduce the three pillars: ruvector-attn-mincut (the fusion seam), ruvector-mincut-gated-transformer (the production transformer that uses the seam), and ruvector-attention (the geometric attention zoo that feeds it).

tour.title: Slice 4 Bridge: The attn-mincut Crate
tour.description: Start at the smallest, most conceptually pure crate: ruvector-attn-mincut. Only nine files — graph_from_logits converts Q·Kᵀ scores into a weighted directed graph, mincut.rs runs Dinic max-flow to discover the cut, gating.rs blends mincut-derived edge masks back into softmax, and hysteresis.rs forces tau consecutive consistent steps before flipping a mask. This is the conceptual fusion point — read it before anything else.
tour.languageLesson: Rust trait dispatch: the attn-mincut crate keeps its surface small by exposing free functions (graph_from_logits, attn_mincut) rather than wide trait hierarchies. When a bridge has one clear job, prefer functions over generics — traits are for polymorphism, not namespacing.

tour.title: Slice 4 Attention: Trait Surface & Crate Root
tour.description: Before the geometric variants, see the contract they all satisfy. traits.rs defines Attention, GraphAttention, GeometricAttention, SparseAttention, TrainableAttention plus shared structs (SparseMask, EdgeInfo, Gradients). lib.rs re-exports the entire ruvector-attention zoo (sheaf, hyperbolic, info-geometry, MoE, PDE, sparse, topology, transport, training, curvature, graph). Together they tell you what 'an attention' MEANS in slice 4: not one algorithm, but a family unified by a trait surface.
tour.languageLesson: Rust associated types let traits speak in terms of 'whatever shape this implementor uses' (e.g. type Output, type Error). Slice-4 attention traits use this pattern so the same trait fits dense f32 tensors and quantized INT8 packed buffers without runtime cost.

tour.title: Slice 4 Attention: Geometric Core
tour.description: The geometric attention family answers 'what KIND of similarity does attention measure?'. Sheaf attention computes Dirichlet energy via per-edge restriction maps (cohomology over a cellular sheaf). Hyperbolic attention replaces Euclidean dot-products with Poincaré-ball distances to encode hierarchies natively. Sliced-Wasserstein uses 1D optimal-transport on random projections for distributional similarity. Mixed-curvature lets different heads live on different manifolds, while PDE-attention models information flow as diffusion.
tour.languageLesson: Rust const generics encode manifold dimensions in the type system: `Poincare<const D: usize>` lets the compiler verify that a 64-dim hyperbolic ball can't be mixed with a 128-dim one. Errors that would be runtime shape mismatches in PyTorch are compile errors here.

tour.title: Slice 4 Attention: Flash, Sparse & Latent KV
tour.description: Where Step 4 was about exotic geometry, this step is about making attention FAST. FlashAttention-3 tiles Q/K/V to keep the working set in cache and uses online softmax (plus Ring Attention for cross-device sharding). Linear attention with kernel feature maps drops cost to O(N). Local+Global sparse attention combines a sliding window with a few global tokens. MLA (DeepSeek-V2/V3 Multi-Head Latent Attention) compresses K/V into a low-rank latent for ~93% KV reduction. ssm.rs is the Mamba SSM non-attention long-range alternative.
tour.languageLesson: Rust SIMD: `std::simd` (portable) and `core::arch::x86_64` (AVX2/AVX-512 intrinsics) let the FlashAttention kernel express tile-wise softmax in vector lanes. Mark hot inner loops `#[inline(always)]` and pin tile sizes with const generics so the autovectorizer can unroll.

tour.title: Slice 4 Attention: Routing, MoE & Topology Gating
tour.description: Attention doesn't just compute — it also DECIDES. MoE router does top-k expert selection with load-balancing loss; moe_attention.rs is the MoE-wrapped attention block. Speculative decoding runs a cheap draft model and validates with the full one. Topology-gated attention is the slice-4 specialty: a coherence signal switches between full / sparse / retrieval-only modes mid-inference, providing the runtime knob that downstream mincut-gating turns.

tour.title: Slice 4 Mincut: Core Algorithm Surface
tour.description: Now flip to the mincut side. ARCHITECTURE.md is the module map; the crate root declares 30 modules. The DynamicGraph in graph/mod.rs is the most-imported file in the slice (fan-in 56) — it is the shared mutable weighted graph backing canonical, dynamic, hierarchy, snn, and optimization modules. canonical/dynamic/mod.rs maintains a min-cut across incremental edge insertions and deletions with periodic full recomputation: this is what gives the attn-mincut bridge its 'dynamic' property.
tour.languageLesson: Rust `&mut` exclusivity: a graph mutated by many algorithms (canonical, snn, optimization) must be borrowed exclusively at each call. The slice-4 mincut crate uses interior mutability (RefCell, Mutex) sparingly and prefers passing `&mut DynamicGraph` because it makes the data dependency explicit at every call site.

tour.title: Slice 4 Mincut: Sparsification & J-Tree
tour.description: The subpolynomial guarantee comes from sparsification. The j-tree (junction tree) is a hierarchical decomposition that lets the dynamic mincut run on a much smaller proxy graph; coordinator.rs orchestrates updates across levels, hierarchy.rs maintains the tree of contracted clusters, sparsifier.rs is the low-recourse Benczur-Karger-style cut sparsifier, and dspar.rs is a degree-based pre-sparsifier. Together these turn a polynomial mincut into something fast enough to call inside an attention head.

tour.title: Slice 4 Mincut: Classical Algorithms & Connectivity
tour.description: The classical mincut machinery that powers the attn-mincut bridge's Dinic call from Step 2. Approximate algorithms (Karger-Stein-style) give probabilistic-correct fast cuts. polylog.rs implements polylogarithmic-time connectivity. localkcut/paper_impl.rs is deterministic local k-cut from the paper. expander/mod.rs is expander decomposition, the structural primitive that makes everything else fast. linkcut/mod.rs is the link-cut tree backing dynamic graph updates.

tour.title: Slice 4 Mincut: SNN Morphogenetic Networks
tour.description: The most exotic mincut feature: a spiking neural network with self-modifying topology. Six layers integrate with dynamic mincut — network.rs is the wiring, attractor.rs and time_crystal.rs are the dynamical regimes, morphogenetic.rs grows new structure under load, and cognitive_engine.rs binds them. The mincut runs continuously to identify weak partitions; the SNN prunes/grows synapses to keep coherence high. This is where mincut becomes a control signal, not just a measurement.

tour.title: Slice 4 Mincut: Runtime, Witness & WASM
tour.description: Algorithms need a runtime. optimization/pool.rs is an arena allocator that keeps mincut hot-path allocations off the global allocator. optimization/parallel.rs is the work-stealing parallel runtime; simd_distance.rs vectorises pair distances. witness/mod.rs is the determinism story: every cut decision emits a signed witness so the audit trail can be replayed. wasm/canonical.rs exposes the same algorithms to browsers and agent runtimes via WASM bindings, so a mincut decision can run in a Cloudflare Worker.
tour.languageLesson: Rust WASM via wasm-bindgen: marking a function `#[wasm_bindgen]` generates a JS shim and serialises arguments through a typed boundary. Slice-4 mincut keeps the WASM surface narrow (canonical.rs, simd.rs, agentic.rs) because every exported function widens the JS attack surface and bloats the .wasm payload.

tour.title: Slice 4 Transformer: Kernels, FlashAttention & Mamba
tour.description: ruvector-mincut-gated-transformer is the production transformer that consumes everything above. THEORY.md explains the coherence-gated math. flash_attention.rs is the FlashAttention-style tiled CPU kernel with online softmax and fused tiled GEMM. mamba.rs is the SSM long-range branch. kernel/qgemm.rs is INT8 matmul. model.rs assembles these into the forward pass.
tour.languageLesson: Rust block-tiled GEMM: const-generic tile sizes (`const TILE_M: usize = 32`) let LLVM unroll the inner three loops completely. Combined with `#[target_feature(enable = "avx2")]` on the hot function, you get hand-tuned assembly without writing it.

tour.title: Slice 4 Transformer: KV Cache Family
tour.description: Modern transformer inference is bottlenecked on KV cache memory bandwidth, not FLOPs. hot_buffer.rs keeps the most recent tokens uncompressed for speed. quantized_store.rs holds older tokens at INT8/INT4. tier.rs orchestrates eviction across hot/warm/cold tiers. kivi.rs implements KIVI 2-bit, kvquant.rs implements KVQuant, squat.rs implements SQuAT. This is what makes long-context inference fit in memory.

tour.title: Slice 4 Transformer: Gate Policies & Tiered Control
tour.description: This is where slice 4's whole thesis lands. gate.rs's GateController fuses energy-gate + config signals into a TierDecision (full / reduced / safe / intervention) — full attention when coherent, sparse when not, retrieval-only when degrading, halt on intervention. energy_gate.rs measures attention energy, sparse_attention.rs is the mincut-derived sparse mask, mod_routing.rs is Mixture-of-Depths token-level routing, and trace.rs tracks lambda-trend so the gate has temporal context.
tour.languageLesson: Rust enum-as-decision: `enum TierDecision { Full, Reduced, Safe, Intervention }` plus exhaustive `match` makes it a compile error to forget a tier when the gate adds a new mode. Encode policy outcomes as enums, not booleans, so the type system enforces coverage.

tour.title: Slice 4 GNN: HNSW + Continual Learning
tour.description: ruvector-gnn is the learning-side companion to the algorithm-side mincut. layer.rs is the GNN message-passing layer running over slice-3's HNSW topology. ewc.rs implements Elastic Weight Consolidation for continual learning without catastrophic forgetting. graphmae.rs is GraphMAE self-supervised pretraining. scheduler.rs detects drift and decides when to retrain. mmap.rs pages features in/out of disk so the GNN can index billions of nodes. The output: embeddings that feed back into the attention/mincut stack.

tour.title: Slice 4 Capstone: Tests, Benches & End-to-End Examples
tour.description: Close the tour by seeing slice 4 run. BENCHMARK_REPORT.md publishes the measured numbers. determinism.rs validates that witness-logged decisions replay bit-identically. paper_algorithms_bench.rs validates the subpolynomial-mincut paper claims. latency.rs benchmarks the gated-transformer hot path. flash_attention_demo.rs is a runnable end-to-end example. This is your starting point for adding a new attention variant or a new mincut algorithm.
